Spec-AC-01.evidence: runPhase1 reads domain map + test files, builds matrix, writes proposal with approved:false, never moves tests. TEST-001 green.

Spec-AC-02.details: No git mv (uses fs.rmSync+fs.writeFileSync instead of git mv). verifyRunner only checks shebang, not actual runner execution. TEST-002/003/004/005 green but test assertions are soft.

Spec-AC-03.details: sourceHashes recorded from original paths. After Phase 2 archives originals, sources no longer exist. Re-run always detects drift because hashTestSources returns null for all. Idempotency broken — every re-run reports drift. TEST-006/007 green but test only checks map byte-identity, not behavioral idempotency.

Spec-AC-04.details: verifyRunner only checks shebang, not actual runner execution. Bidirectional back-links incomplete: archive→canonical present, but canonical→archive missing (renderCanonicalTest has no archive back-link). TEST-008 green but assertions are soft.

Spec-AC-05.evidence: runPhase1 degrades gracefully when docs/canonical/ absent. TEST-009 green.

Spec-AC-06.evidence: runPhase1 writes human-readable coverage report. TEST-010 green.

Spec-AC-07.evidence: Stubs carry domain+criterion tags, syntactically valid bash. TEST-011 green.

Spec-AC-08.details: --drift reports without modifying files. But --resync reads from original paths that no longer exist after Phase 2 — sourceContents is empty, canonical loses all source content. Should read from archived sources. TEST-012 green but resync produces empty canonical.

errors.message: Drift detection always triggers after Phase 2 because d.sources still points to originals that were archived and deleted. hashTestSources returns null for all, so every re-run detects drift. Re-run is not idempotent — behavior differs on every invocation.
errors.fix: Update d.sources to point to archived paths after moving, OR hash the archived copies, OR hash the canonical test content instead.

errors.message: --resync reads source contents from original paths that no longer exist after Phase 2 archiving. sourceContents will be empty, producing a canonical test with only stubs and no real test content. Spec requires re-synthesizing from archived sources.
errors.fix: On --resync, read from tests/_archive/ paths using d.archivedAt mapping instead of d.sources paths.

warnings.message: Uses fs.writeFileSync + fs.rmSync instead of git mv for archiving originals. Spec-AC-02 requires 'tracked git move'. Git may not detect the rename, losing file history.
warnings.fix: Use `git mv` or `execSync('git mv ...')` to preserve rename tracking in git history.

warnings.message: verifyRunner only checks shebang presence. Spec-AC-04 requires verifying the canonical suite 'still runs via existing runners'. No actual test execution through Pester, test-framework.sh, or test-*.sh runners.
warnings.fix: Execute each canonical test through the project's existing runners and assert exit 0 before archiving.

warnings.message: renderCanonicalTest does not include archive back-links. Spec-AC-04 requires bidirectional back-links (archive→canonical AND canonical→archive). Only archive files get '# Canonical:' pointer.
warnings.fix: Add '# Archive: tests/_archive/...' lines to the canonical test file header for each archived source.

info.message: mapTestToDomain uses heuristic name matching (slugClean/nameClean) which may produce incorrect domain assignments. The 'unclear' bucket + HITL review mitigate this per spec design, but the heuristic is coarse.
info.fix: Consider using content-based matching (e.g., grep for domain references in test comments/frontmatter) in addition to name matching.

info.message: extractCriteria only parses bullet items under '## Acceptance Criteria' heading. May miss criteria in other formats (tables, numbered lists, sub-headings).
info.fix: Consider broader parsing for alternative criterion formats.

info.message: parseArgs defaults to --phase1 when no flag given. This is reasonable but could surprise users who invoke without args expecting help output.
info.fix: Consider printing usage/help when no recognized args are given.

verdict_reason: Stage 1: 4 Spec-AC non-compliant (Spec-AC-02, Spec-AC-03, Spec-AC-04, Spec-AC-08). Stage 2: 2 ERROR findings (E1: drift always triggers, E2: --resync reads non-existent paths).
recommendation: Fix E1 (drift hash against archived paths), fix E2 (resync from archived sources), address W1 (git mv), W2 (real runner verification), W3 (bidirectional back-links). Then re-review.
